name: Billy Childs
abstract: William Edward Childs (born March 8, 1957) is an American composer, jazz pianist, arranger, and conductor from Los Angeles, California. Childs's In the Arms of the Beloved was a finalist for the 2026 Pulitzer Prize for Music.

works_created.t: Each Moment Is a New Discovery

about_quotes.speaker: Dianne Reeves
about_quotes.evidence: We started working together when we were about 19 years old... I asked him would he do the arrangements... I remember when I had a band with Billy Childs
about_quotes.doc_title: Dianne Reeves interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, October 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: So my first group... was Billy Higgins, was the drummer, Billy Childs was the pianist
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Hyman
about_quotes.evidence: I was very touched last year when James Williams and what’s his name Childs, the other pianist... They called me up together and wished me a happy birthday
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Hyman interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I suggested both Billy Childs and Bill Banfield, who constitute the rest of the recording.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alicia Olatuja
about_quotes.evidence: I did a piece with Billy Childs... And we performed it at Michigan State.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alicia Olatuja interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 9, 2018
